feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Data Migration by creating copies. Best Approach?
entry.content:
I have a client that needs to replace all Material Master data with copies of new Materials (This is being done to enable Batch Split Valuation). So after creating new materials, we need to create new BOMs, recipes, production versions, inspection plans, Batch masters, Move Stock, purchase info records, sales conditions records. Can someone help me out with identifying BAPIs for this process. I need to be able to extract the existing data, change the material code and upload the new data.

I've found BAPI/FM for some of the master datas

1. Material Master - BAPI_MATERIAL_GETALL , BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEREPLICA , BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA

2. BOM - CSAP_MAT_BOM_READ , CSAP_MAT_BOM_MAINTAIN

3. Recipe -

4. Production Version -

5. Batches -

6. Stock - BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE , BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT

7. Purchase Info Record -

8. Sales Condition Record -

entry.title: Material Sales Org Distr Channel Customer not found
entry.content:
When attempting to transfer data directly from ECC to S4, I get the errors, "Material XXX in sales org. 1030 distribution channel 10 is not defined" and "Material Sales Org Distr Channel Customer not found".  The material does exist in S4 in that sales org/dc.  I am not quite sure about the 2nd error, since the customer has also been extended to the 1030/10 sales org./dc.

In addition, for some records I got the message Text type “” has not been defined

However, I am able to create the above directly with the fiori app/SAP GUI.

Anyone came across this?

entry.title: Migration Cockpit for migrate from SAP TM Cloud extra stack
entry.content:
Does anyone know if we can use a Migration Cockpit for migrate from SAP TM Cloud extra stack to TM 

embedded SAP S/4HANA?

entry.title: Business Partner : Contact Person : Standard Relationship : how to set in Data Migration or CVI ?
entry.content:
Dear All,

Context :

  • Data Migration of customers : ECC 6.0 --> S/4HANA (using Migration Cockpit - Direct Approach)
  • The Contact Persons and the relationships are well migrated. 

However, I do not see a way to set the flag "Standard Relationship" (which is field BUT050-XDFREL) in the migration object "Customer".

How can I set the flag "Standard Relationship" in Data Migration (or in the CVI setup ?)

entry.title: Migration Cockpit Internal numbers mapping table
entry.content:
Hello,

We have loaded some materials using internal numbers in the Cockpit, which is fine.  However, we'd like to get a definitive mapping of the dummy numbers used in the load file (1,2,3, etc) to the ones appearing in MARA (70012345, 70012346, etc.).  Sadly, the system has jumbled them up (in blocks) so it's not just a case of referencing the last 1400 materials created in order.  (I know they are out of alignment as I have done an XLOOKUP on the material text.)  Where can we get this mapping, please?

Many thanks.

entry.title: Function module CNV_PE_S4_RFCFM_ORG_DERIV missing?
entry.content:
Hi,

my collegue is configuring a migration project on our S/4H 2022 onPremise server.

Migration ApproachMigrate Data Directly from SAP System
ScenarioSAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA
Connection to Source System<RFC connection to ERP6.0 EHP8 with IDES>

The process stops with an error message (see attachment) after clicking "create project". There is an error with no detail regarding the function module CNV_PE_S4_RFCFM_ORG_DERIV. This function module does only exist on the S/4H system, but not on the ERP system. Do we have to install something on the ERP system?

Best Regards,

Christian

entry.title: LTMOM Migration Cockpit - how to filter on one table with two fields in key?
entry.content: I would like to ask how to migrate standard PM data (table like PLKO) where in the key fields in source tables are two fields - I have a list of documents to be migrated, how can I filter them using two fields in filter? Is there only option to have the documents listed in new Z Table, and to use BADI either in selection in source system, or in skip rule with ABAP code in destination system?

entry.title: EHS Data Migration for SAP S/4hana on-premise
entry.content:
Hi,

We want to migrate the data of the EHS module in the S/4hana on-premise system using the staging table method—data such as chemical substance, risk, control, and compliance requirements for OEL. There is no standard template for these data in the Migration Cockpit app. I would be grateful if anyone has a solution for this problem.

Regards

entry.title: Data Migration Cockpit Master Recipe template uses non-standard field codes
entry.content:
With all the other DMC templates, the technical field name used can be found in the relevant table (even if you have to hunt a bit for the table).  They are all there.  However, for this one pack, they are very different.  For example, for the Receipe Group, the field in the pack is MASTERRECIPEGROUP and not PLNNR in table PLKO, PLPO, etc.  When the data was originally loaded, a custom template was used (which in itself is odd, as it is the only one) which I have.  This contains the real codes, so for example PLNNR is used.  

So, has anyone actually used this standard Master Recipe template and, if so, did it work fine or was a huge amount of mapping needed?

Thanks.

entry.title: Error in uploading GL's in S4 Hana
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing the error while uploading the gl's in S4Hana using app.

Please provide the solution for that issue.

 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: FTXP Maintain Tax Rates - Do we have Data migration cockpit object available in S4 HANA?
entry.content:
Hi, We are on S4 HANA 2023 version and looking for ways to load tax rates for tax conditions (FTXP) 

I have looked at Condition record for pricing (general template). This is not working as we need to maintain condition Key fields. 

Is there any other DMC object that is delivered for this? Any other ways to load?

entry.title: How to import LTMC Migration Projects to the Migration Cockpit Fiori app
entry.content:
Dear experts,

Our team has recently upgraded our SAP S/4HANA 2020 to SAP S/4HANA 2023, and after doing so, we noticed that the Migration Projects shown in transaction LTMC don't show up in its new corresponding Fiori app (Migrate Your Data - Migration Cockpit).

We can still see our Migration Projects in LTMC and use the new Fiori app without any issues, but we'd like to know:

  • Is there a way of importing the Migration Projects in transaction LTMC to the Migrate Your Data Fiori app?

Thanks a lot for your support!

Best regards,
Data Migration team


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Extract SAP long text into SQL server
entry.content:
Hi, Is there a way to read SAP long text for e.g. BOM long text, routing long text etc...

We are using SQL Sever to extract and transform sap data and then load in to S/4 HANA. Since the long text is stored in STXH and STXL,  and STXL contain text in RAW format, it is not possible to extract using SQL.

SAP Data services or Syniti tool is not an option for us.

Please let me know if there is a way to extract sap long text into SQL server

entry.title: Missing packages for S/4 Migration in Maintenance Planner
entry.content:
Hello friends.

When I upload my XML in the Maintenance Planner and plan an S4 migration, I get the message that packages are missing. How do I proceed now? Do I have to wait until the next release?

Missing packages:

SAPK-10802INS4CEXT
SAPK-10802INS4CORE
SAPK-10802INS4COREOP
SAPK-10802INS4DEPREC
SAPK-10802INS4FND

entry.title: ADT : Error trying to open object of ATC remote check
entry.content:
Hi experts

Context:
We have configured the Fiori application Abap Custom Code Migration on a Hana system SXX, and successfully executed a Central check on the ECC referenced system DYY.

After configuring ADT, I'm able to see the results of the ATC check in the ATC Result Browser tab, in section "Results of Central Checks Runs.

But once in the details section, I'm facing the following issues: 

1/ Trying to open the development objects

I have the error:

2/ Trying the recheck

I have the error

 

3/ No option to open the check in ATC Problems view. 

I have no dumps in ST22, nor explicit messages in SM21.

Many thanks for your help
Regads
Pierre

entry.title: Can you provide suggestions for migrating data from SAP R/3 (4.7) to SAP S/4HANA Cloud (public)?
entry.content:
Hi ,
Aside from the staging table approach, can data migration be achieved using standard iFlows in SAP Cloud Platform Integration, or would we need to create custom iFlows or custom programs for this purpose?

Are there any other methods or third-party tools that can be used?

entry.title: Migration Cockpit - A basic guide
entry.content:
I have created this guide to show the basic steps to create and execute a project using the Migration Cockpit.  It was documented using S/4 Hana 2022 On Premise.

Configuration within each system will vary so these steps may need to be modified to address your needs but this basic scenario will help you to get started.

Migration Cockpit

Fiori App F3473 Migrate Your Data

Information on standard user roles required to complete these steps can be found in the Fiori Apps Library for the mentioned app.

 

Example Scenario

In this example I will assign the Class “CONTROLLER” to Material 000000000000000015 with value “NAV_COMM” for Characteristic “AVIONICS”

Material Classification view before migration shows there is no Class assigned currently:

 

Create Project

Create your project using Staging Tables.  This will allow for import of data from excel template files.

Migrating Data Directly from SAP System requires RFC connection set up to the source system and would be used for Extraction of data from that source system.

 

 

Select the objects in scope.  Note there may be more than one version of an object.  In the case of Classifications there is the general template and the object specific templates.  Select objects by checking the box to the left and then use the right arrow in the middle of the screen to add them to your scope. Then click on the Review button

 

Where relevant, you will receive a warning regarding predecessor objects.  It is not always necessary to include each of these in your scope as you may be managing them via alternative methods, including manually, or they may already exist – perhaps your migration project is simply to update existing objects.   Review the list and update your scope as necessary.

Review your settings, edit if necessary and then click Create Project.

 

Edit Project

It is possible to edit some of the settings you made in the Create Project step but there are also some additional settings that can be made.

From the home screen select your project and then click Edit

In General Data you can rename your project and define Custom Scope

In Migration Objects you can change the scope of objects

In CSV File Options you can manage some technical settings for the file import

In Technical Information you can define the retention period for the data in the staging tables.

 

Managing Your Project

Enter your project to begin managing each object

For each object there is a set list of steps or Actions.  Select your object and then the next Action from the dropdown menu.   Each Action is described in the following sections.

 

Download Template

There are two options for downloading the object templates:  xml and csv.  The xml file is actually presented in Excel and includes instructions, a field list (which can also be used as the basis of a field mapping document) and a separate sheet for each sub object.   These correlate to the sections in the field list, which are tables in the structure.

The csv option will provide you with a zip file containing the csv files for each of the sub objects/tables.  These correlate back to each sheet in the xml.

The xml is a little more user friendly.

In this screenshot you can see that the Header sheet in the xml is managed as a separate csv file in the master zip file. 

 

 

Upload Template

When choosing the Upload Template action, in addition to uploading your csv or xml files – or drag and dropping them – you can also review the staging table structure and review documentation about the migration object.  This is very useful information.

Drag and drop or upload your completed template.  The system will validate and notify you of issues.  Once resolved it should confirm successful import of the file.

 

Prepare

If you are using standard template files the system checks the consistency of the data in the staging tables but it will provide the option to perform this check at this point for those who imported data via another tool.

Once you choose to Prepare Staging Tables you can monitor progress via the Monitoring button in menu bar

 

 

Resolve any errors before proceeding to Mapping Tasks.  You may need to delete the uploaded data, correct the issue in the file and then re-upload.

 

Mapping Tasks

You can proceed to the Mapping Tasks step directly from the previous step as shown in previous screenshot or from the home screen for the project.

You now need to map your data :

For each attribute, confirm the source to target mapping.  The system will default the target value to be the same as the source value.  You can overwrite this if you need to.  Each value must be Confirmed, even if you are accepting the defaulted value.

 

 

Simulate

You will now perform a simulation run to validate the data load and expectations.  It can be run for all instances, random or custom selections.  Errors will be presented and must be resolved until your simulation run is clean and “Migrate” is available as the next Action.

 

Migrate

Click on the Action value “Migrate” and then choose which records to process.  Start your migration.

As before, the screen will refresh periodically to show progress but you can also choose the Monitoring button to get up to date progress.  Final status will be presented to the screen.

All data was successfully migrated and we can now check the results.

Material Classification view after migration, shows Class and Characteristic value assigned.

The Action step reverts to “Upload” to allow you to run additional files.  Note, you will need to map any new values that appear in the source data each time.  It is possible to enhance the mapping behavior of each object within a migration project using Gui transaction LTMOM which is the Migration Object Modeler.   This would be considered more of a technical task.

 

Finishing a Project

When all migration activities are completed for a project it is expected that you will mark it as Finished.  This will prevent any further activities being performed or changes being made.  It will also begin the countdown with regard to the data retention period.

If you are creating a project for ongoing upload of data then do not set it to Finished.

entry.title: How to update equipments using the Migration Cockpit app?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

Is it possible to update equipments using the Migration Cockpit? The Migration Object "PM - Equipment" allows us to create equipments in our system, but we'd like to know if there's a way of updating the existing ones using the Migrate Your Data app.

If the update isn't possible, could you explain us why?

Thanks in advance!

Best regards,
Data migration expert team


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Will the data migration from ECC to SAP S/4 Hana include the data from link tables and the AIS?
entry.content:
I'm new to SAP migration. I would like to know if we decide to use a data migration strategy such as the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit, which uses an RFC connection to transfer data directly from SAP ECC 6.0 to SAP S/4HANA. Is the data migration from ECC 6.0 to SAP S/4 Hana taking into account link tables like ADMI_FILES and indexed data from the archive information structure?

Thanks

entry.title: Migration of SD - Condition Records for Free Goods
entry.content:
Hi Members, 

I am migrating the condition records for free goods using the SD - Condition Records for Free Goods Migration Object in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition. During the migration, I need to input the condition table number for the condition type YA00 - Free Goods SD. However, I couldn't find the condition table number for the mentioned condition type in the system.

Then, when I simulate the migration project, it says, "For condition type YA00 and table 004, there are no valid access sequences."

I'm trying to find the KB but couldn't find any. Please help me with where I can check the condition table number and access sequence for the condition type YA00 - Free Goods SD.

Thanks.

Kenny
